title: Research assistant with task management adjacency
summary: An AI assistant pattern that combines web search, task management, and research paper analysis.
why_interesting: Validates adjacent demand for research plus task workflows, but needs a sharper experiment-selection wedge.
implied_experiment: Prototype candidate cards that convert source notes into explicit next experiments and risks.
acceptance_check: A founder can choose one card and explain the next experiment in under 10 seconds.

score_reasons.novelty: Known assistant pattern, sharper when focused on candidate decisions.
score_reasons.feasibility: Static card prototype is testable in a day.
score_reasons.leverage: Creates reusable card schema for later extraction work.
score_reasons.evidence: Single repo signal needs manual validation.

risks: Could become a generic assistant instead of a decision-quality tool

strongest_signals: Adjacent demand for research plus task workflows

trace.rationale: Use this as an adjacent-workflow source, not as proof that a generic assistant should be built.
reviewer_notes: Candidate may proceed because the source is candidate_ready and duplicate check is complete.

title: Code as Agent Harness
summary: Frames agents as code, harnesses, and evaluable execution units.
why_interesting: Matches the need to judge candidates by concrete harnesses and acceptance criteria, not just summaries.
implied_experiment: Add an experiment brief template that requires harness, fixture, and acceptance criteria fields.
acceptance_check: Every promoted brief names the harness, fixture, and pass/fail signal.

score_reasons.feasibility: Template work is small and isolated.
score_reasons.evidence: Paper signal is strong but still early.

risks: May overfit to agent experiments before validating broader research workflows

strongest_signals: Executable harness framing maps directly to experiment brief acceptance criteria
weakest_signals: May be too agent-specific for broader research workflows

trace.rationale: Use this source to require concrete harness, fixture, and acceptance criteria fields before implementation work.
reviewer_notes: Candidate may proceed because the source is candidate_ready and evidence includes a strong paper-method signal.

title: Structured signals outperform generative matching in reviewer selection
summary: A reminder that structured representations can outperform generative AI for matching/scoring tasks.
why_interesting: Supports combining LLM summaries with source metadata, recency, tags, and keyword overlap.
implied_experiment: Compare manual scoring against a simple structured scoring baseline before adding LLM recommendations.
acceptance_check: Baseline scoring is compared against one manual shortlist decision.

score_reasons.novelty: Known structured-signal lesson applied to this workflow.
score_reasons.feasibility: Can be evaluated with existing candidate data.
score_reasons.leverage: Provides a cheap baseline before LLM ranking.

risks: Structured signals may be unavailable for manually entered sources

strongest_signals: Structured scoring is a clear baseline before LLM-only recommendations
weakest_signals: Manual sources may not provide complete metadata

trace.rationale: Use this source to keep scoring deterministic and visible before LLM recommendation features are introduced.
reviewer_notes: Candidate may proceed because the source is candidate_ready and evidence score does not exceed the source strength ceiling.

title: Manual note clustering for founder research
summary: Cluster rough notes into candidate project cards.
why_interesting: Manual sources are common, but extraction confidence is uneven.
implied_experiment: Review extracted fields before ranking the manual-note card.
acceptance_check: Reviewer can correct missing fields before score/rank controls are enabled.

title: Rubric calibration baseline
summary: Use a small set of known-good examples to calibrate rubric scores.
why_interesting: Would make later shortlist decisions more consistent.
implied_experiment: Score five seed candidates and compare ranking against founder preference.
acceptance_check: All score dimensions have a reason before promotion.

title: Saved shortlist export retry
summary: Keep ranked shortlist state visible even if export fails.
why_interesting: Protects explicit promotion decisions from async failures.
implied_experiment: Simulate export failure and verify rank, rationale, and draft brief stay visible.
acceptance_check: Export failure leaves rank and draft copy visible for retry/manual copy.

score_reasons.evidence: Design artifact calls out this edge.

risks: Export retry could be mistaken for implementation handoff

title: Agent board duplicate candidate
summary: Potential overlap with an existing task-board experiment.
why_interesting: Useful only if it differs from the existing board workflow.
implied_experiment: Compare duplicate evidence before merging or rejecting.
acceptance_check: Duplicate comparison is visible and no automatic merge occurs.

score_reasons.novelty: Likely overlaps with an existing candidate.

title: Conflicting evidence runner
summary: Sources disagree on whether the project should be research-first or prototype-first.
why_interesting: Forces the UI to expose disagreement instead of averaging it away.
implied_experiment: Review source-level disagreement and choose research or prototype lane.
acceptance_check: Conflict copy is visible and promotion requires rationale.

title: Dataset browser for future sourcing
summary: A useful future data-browser idea that is too broad for this cycle.
why_interesting: May support sourcing later, but not the immediate shortlist interaction.
implied_experiment: Revisit after core candidate flow is validated.
acceptance_check: Parked reason is visible and restore action is available.

score_reasons.feasibility: Too broad for a short experiment.

parked_reason: Too broad for the current cycle.

title: Social monitor as generic trend feed
summary: A generic social trend monitor without a specific experiment-selection wedge.
why_interesting: Out of scope for the current lab unless tied to candidate evidence.
implied_experiment: Restore only if a source-specific experiment is defined.
acceptance_check: Rejected card is muted, keeps reason, and can be restored.
reject_reason: Too generic for the current shortlist cycle.

title: Failed fetch fallback card
summary: A source URL was saved, but automatic fetching failed.
why_interesting: Ensures source loss does not erase user input.
implied_experiment: Retry fetch or enter details manually without clearing the URL.
acceptance_check: Saved URL remains visible through retry and manual-entry states.

score_reasons.evidence: Fetch failed, so evidence is weak.
